etymology.description: Inverted „ěĘ (originally depicting a footstep) -> ŚłÄ (represents surrounding)
etymology.interpretation: The character ŚłÄ depicts an inverted form of the character „ěĘ, associated with footsteps, which aligns with the meaning of 'to surround' as it visually suggests encircling. This connection emphasizes the action of enclosing or surrounding a space as footsteps might mark a boundary.
